metadata.methodology: Manual extraction from forward-looking statements per prompts/01_gather_commitments.md. Earnings call transcripts (Q3 2024 through Q4 2025), Investor Day materials, and acquisition announcements analyzed.
metadata.qa_weighting: Q&A responses weighted higher than prepared remarks per methodology. Seeking Alpha, Insider Monkey, and third-party transcript summaries used where full transcripts were paywalled.
metadata.limitations: Full earnings call transcripts for Q1 2025 and Q2 2025 only partially accessible (Seeking Alpha paywall). Investor Day presentation slides (S-002) accessed via summaries and 6-K filing descriptions rather than direct slide content. Some Q&A-specific quotes may be paraphrased from summary sources rather than verbatim.

commitments.statement: For 2025, we are targeting $6 billion of fundraising and $200 to $225 million of FRE, with FRE margins between 58% to 60%.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: FY2025 actual: $7.7B fundraising (exceeded $6B by 28%), FRE $202.5M (within $200-225M range), FRE margin 58.9% (within 58-60% target). All three metrics met or exceeded.
commitments.notes: Initial 2025 guidance set at Q4 2024 earnings call. Fundraising target subsequently raised mid-year. FRE landed at low end of range but within guidance.

commitments.statement: Three-year targets (2025-2027): $6B+ annual fundraising growing to $8B by 2027, $200M+ FRE growing to $260-290M by 2027, FEAUM reaching $70B by end of 2027, cumulative $21B in organic fundraising, performance-related earnings of $120-140M over 2024-2027.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: Year 1 (2025) exceeded targets on fundraising ($7.7B vs $6B) and met FRE ($202.5M). FEAUM at $40.8B vs $70B 3-year target. 2026 and 2027 remain to be delivered. $7.7B of $21B cumulative target achieved (37%).
commitments.notes: Investor Day December 9, 2024. Management stated only ~10% of 2027 FRE target expected from acquisitions, implying primarily organic growth. Filed as 6-K with SEC (S-025).

commitments.statement: 2026 FRE guidance: $225 million to $245 million ($1.42 to $1.54 per share), with FRE margins of 58% to 60%.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO / CFO
commitments.status_evidence: Guidance first introduced at Q3 2025 earnings call and reiterated at Q4 2025. 2026 is in progress; no quarterly results yet available for 2026.
commitments.notes: Represents 11-21% growth over 2025 actual of $202.5M. Reiterated at Q4 2025 earnings call (S-007).

commitments.statement: 2026 organic fundraising target of $7 billion.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: Set at Q4 2025 earnings call. CEO noted this is conservative and will reassess after H1 2026. 2025 actual was $7.7B, so $7B target implies moderation.
commitments.notes: CEO described guidance as conservative, preferring to reassess after first two quarters. Implies potential for upward revision similar to 2025 pattern ($6B -> $6.3-6.6B -> $7.7B actual).

commitments.statement: 2027 FRE target of $260 million to $290 million ($1.60 to $1.80 per share), with fundraising target of $8 billion and FEAUM target of $70 billion.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: 2027 targets maintained through all subsequent earnings calls. Current FEAUM of $40.8B (end 2025) needs to reach $70B by end 2027, requiring ~72% growth in 2 years (organic + M&A). Year 1 exceeded expectations, building credibility.
commitments.notes: Only ~10% of 2027 FRE target expected from acquisitions. FEAUM target of $70B is ambitious from $40.8B base — implies significant organic growth plus further M&A.

commitments.statement: We raised our full-year fundraising expectation to 5% to 10% above our original $6 billion target, implying $6.3 to $6.6 billion.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: FY2025 actual fundraising: $7.7B, exceeding even the raised target of $6.3-6.6B by 17-22%. Upward revision validated.
commitments.notes: Mid-year guidance raise from $6B to $6.3-6.6B. Actual of $7.7B exceeded even the raised target significantly.

commitments.statement: Patria is off to a very exciting start to 2025 as fundraising totaled a record $3.2 billion, and we are confident in achieving three-year targets introduced December 9th, despite acknowledged trade and economic headwinds.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: Year 1 results validate confidence: 2025 exceeded all targets. 2026-2027 delivery remains to be demonstrated.
commitments.notes: Noteworthy that CEO acknowledged headwinds (trade/economic) while maintaining confidence. Q1 2025 included ~$1B from Asian SWF investors.

commitments.statement: Platform diversification is paying off in terms of fundraising and profitable organic growth.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: Validated by record $7.7B fundraising across diversified asset classes (PE, Infrastructure, Credit, Real Estate, GPMS) and FRE margin expansion to 58.9%. No single segment dominates fundraising.
commitments.notes: Recurring theme across all earnings calls. Fundraising from 'platforms that did not exist at IPO' per CEO.

commitments.statement: We have almost $3.5 billion of already committed capital that should turn into fee-earning AUM as the capital is deployed over the next 4 to 6 quarters, with significant progress anticipated within 2025.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: FEAUM grew from ~$35B (Q1 2025) to $40.8B (Q4 2025), implying significant deployment. Q3 2025 still showed >$3B pending FEAUM for 12-18 month deployment. Partial progress demonstrated.
commitments.notes: 4-6 quarter timeline from Q1 2025 extends through Q2-Q3 2026. FEAUM growth of ~$7.8B in FY2025 (+24%) suggests substantial deployment occurred.

commitments.statement: Solis acquisition expected to close in Q1 2026 and be accretive to FRE and distributable earnings in the first year.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: Solis closed January 2, 2026 (within Q1 2026 timeline). Accretion assessment requires 2026 results but management confirmed expectation at Q4 earnings call.
commitments.notes: Rapid close timeline (Nov 26 announcement -> Jan 2 close = 37 days). Founders continue leading the platform.

commitments.statement: RBR Gestao acquisition expected to close in Q1 2026, accretive to FRE and DE in year one. Makes Patria the leading REIT manager in Brazil.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: RBR closed in February 2026, within Q1 2026 timeline. No shareholder or antitrust approvals were required, facilitating rapid close.
commitments.notes: Post-deal, permanent capital vehicles represent ~22% of total FEAUM. Pro-forma REIT FEAUM: $5.7B.

commitments.statement: Credit to become more than 25% of total FEAUM following the Solis acquisition.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: Pro-forma Credit FEAUM exceeds $11.7B post-Solis close (Jan 2, 2026). Total FEAUM $40.8B + Solis $3.5B + RBR $1.3B = ~$45.6B pro-forma. Credit at $11.7B/$45.6B = ~26%. Target met.
commitments.notes: Credit was a small segment at IPO (primarily Moneda). Grew from ~$5B (Moneda at 2021 combination) to $12.1B pro-forma (post-Solis). Structural shift in business mix.

commitments.statement: Dividend increased to $0.65 per share annually for 2026, representing an 8% increase. Board expects total payout of approximately $100M.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: Dividend announced at Q4 2025 earnings. First 2026 payment declared. Full year delivery requires four quarterly payments through 2026.
commitments.notes: Progressive dividend: $0.60/share (2025) -> $0.65/share (2026). Implies ~$100M total payout at ~158M share count.

commitments.statement: Share buyback expanded to total 7 million shares authorized. Patria Partners intends to purchase up to 2.5 million PAX shares. Share count expected to average 158 to 160 million through 2027.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: 1.5M shares acquired via TRS in Q3 2025 (settlement by Q3 2026). Additional 3M shares authorized in Q4 2025. Patria Partners purchase commitment announced. Ongoing execution through 2026-2027.
commitments.notes: Progressive expansion of capital return: initial 3M shares (Aug 2025) -> TRS 1.5M -> additional 3M + 2.5M insider. Total 7M shares = ~4.4% of outstanding shares.

commitments.statement: Patria is in a strong position to achieve, and hopefully exceed, the three-year fundraising and FRE objectives set at our December 2024 Investor Day.
commitments.speaker: Alex Saigh, CEO
commitments.status_evidence: Year 1 strongly validates: fundraising exceeded by 28%, FRE within range, margins within target. But 2026-2027 still ahead. Use of 'hopefully exceed' suggests aspiration beyond baseline targets.
commitments.notes: Upgraded from 'confident' (Q1-Q2 2025) to 'even more confident' (Q3 2025) to 'strong position to achieve and hopefully exceed' (Q4 2025). Increasing conviction over time.

commitments.statement: WP Global Partners acquisition signed; expected to be accretive to FRE and DE in year one. Pro-forma GPMS FEAUM exceeds $13.3B with ~40% in U.S. assets.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: Deal signed Feb 2, 2026. Closing timeline not specified in press release. Subject to customary conditions. Year-one accretion depends on close timing.
commitments.notes: First U.S. acquisition. All-cash at 1.7% of FEAUM + earnout (2029). Represents strategic entry into U.S. lower-middle-market PE solutions.

commitments.statement: Preparing next vintage of Latin America private credit strategy, expected to raise more than $500 million, building on first fund's 15.6% IRR performance.
commitments.speaker: Javier Montero, Partner and Head of Private Credit
commitments.status_evidence: Fund preparation announced Feb 11, 2026. Target >$500M per Bloomberg reporting. First fund closed at $314M, so >$500M would represent 60%+ increase. Fundraising not yet commenced as of announcement.
commitments.notes: Market tailwinds: private credit <1% of LatAm's $2.3T corporate credit system. LatAm corporate leverage 2.8x vs 5.5x in US HY. First fund >70% deployed into 14 transactions.

commitments.statement: Tria energy trading platform signed definitive agreement to acquire Raizen Power. Expected to become one of Brazil's largest independent energy traders.
commitments.speaker: Management
commitments.status_evidence: Definitive agreement signed Dec 19, 2025. Subject to CADE (Brazil antitrust) approval. Closing pending regulatory clearance. Tria contributed $4M to 2025 DE.
commitments.notes: Management described 'excitement regarding the long-term potential of this business.' Tria has revenue >R$1.5B, equity ~R$200M. Launched in 2024. Infrastructure adjacency play.

commitments.statement: Management fee rate expected to trend toward approximately 90 basis points from current 92 basis point average.
commitments.speaker: CFO
commitments.status_evidence: Directional guidance provided at Q4 2025 earnings call. Reflects mix shift toward larger, potentially lower-fee vehicles and credit/GPMS products. Not a specific timeline provided.
commitments.notes: 2bps compression from 92bps to 90bps is modest. May reflect mix shift toward permanent capital and larger institutional mandates rather than fee cuts.

commitments.statement: Peru office expected to obtain operational license from local regulators and open in Lima's commercial district in 2026, becoming Patria's sixth Latin American office.
commitments.speaker: Alfonso Duval, Commercial Director (Andean Region)
commitments.status_evidence: Announced January 29, 2026. License application in progress. No confirmation of opening date yet.
commitments.notes: Reported by Bloomberg (S-033). Signals continued LatAm geographic expansion beyond Brazil, Chile, Colombia. Led by Duval and Felipe Claro (portfolio manager).

key_themes: Consistently met or exceeded guidance: 2024 fundraising ($5.5B vs $5B target), 2025 fundraising ($7.7B vs $6B->$6.6B target), 2025 FRE ($202.5M within $200-225M range)
Escalating confidence: Management language upgraded from 'confident' to 'even more confident' to 'strong position to achieve and hopefully exceed' across 2025 earnings calls
Three-year Investor Day plan (2025-2027) is the strategic anchor: $21B fundraising, $70B FEAUM, $260-290M FRE by 2027
Aggressive capital return: dividend +8%, buyback expanded to 7M shares (4.4% of outstanding), insider purchase commitment
M&A integration timelines consistently met: Solis closed in 37 days, RBR within Q1 2026, Moneda integration completed after 3 years
Credit expansion as most dynamic commitment: from zero dedicated fund to $12.1B pro-forma FEAUM, >25% of total, second vintage preparing
Zero contradicted or silently abandoned commitments identified — unusually strong commitment follow-through
Gap: No comparative framing commitments extracted. Management generally avoids direct competitor comparisons.
